tickets.subject: [Contact Form] Want to donate toys and blankets but only see money option
tickets.description: Hi! My kids and I love PawFinder and we visit the Happy Paws shelter almost every weekend. We have a bunch of dog toys, blankets, and some gently used pet beds that our dog outgrew and we'd love to donate them to the shelter animals. But when I go to the Donate page on your website, the only option is to give money. Don't get me wrong, monetary donations are great, but we specifically want to send physical items — toys, blankets, food, that kind of thing. It would be amazing if there was an option on the website to arrange a physical donation, like pick from a list of needed items and either drop them off or mail them in. I bet a lot of people have old pet supplies sitting around that they'd happily donate if it was easy to do. Could you add something like that? Even just a simple form with a shipping address and a list of what the shelter currently needs would be awesome. Thank you for everything you do for these animals!

tickets.subject: [Contact Form] Submitted adoption application 2 weeks ago — no update at all
tickets.description: I submitted an adoption application for Biscuit (the golden retriever mix) on June 5th and received the confirmation email. Since then, absolutely nothing. No update, no email, no way to check the status on the website. I've tried emailing the shelter directly but haven't heard back. I understand you're probably busy, but it's been two weeks and I have no idea if my application is being reviewed, if someone else adopted Biscuit, or if I need to do something else. The website doesn't have any way to track my application status — I just have to wait and hope for an email. This is really frustrating. A simple status page where I can enter my application number and see where things stand would go a long way. I really want to give Biscuit a good home and the uncertainty is stressful.

tickets.subject: [Contact Form] Would love more photos and maybe video of the animals
tickets.description: First off, your website is great and I found my cat Luna through PawFinder last year. Just a suggestion — it would be wonderful if the animal profile pages had more photos and maybe a short video clip. When I was browsing, some animals only had one photo and it was hard to get a sense of their personality. A 15-second video of them playing or just being themselves would be so helpful for potential adopters who can't visit the shelter in person right away. I know this is probably a lot of work with storage and everything, but even allowing volunteers to upload phone videos would be amazing. Also the gallery on the profile page could use a bit of love — the photos are small and there's no way to swipe through them on mobile. Just some thoughts from a happy adopter!
